title: pinus montana subsp. frutescens-erecta
intro: Pinus montana subsp. frutescens-erecta, also known as the erect white pine, is a subspecies of the white pine tree, native to the Himalayan region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Pinus montana subsp. frutescens-erecta include its ability to reduce inflammation, boost the immune system, and provide relief from coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of its needles, which can be consumed as teas or used in topical applications for medicinal purposes.
